name: Turkey Breast Pinwheels
recipeIngredient: Sprigs of fresh dill, or Dill pickles; cut thin into spears
1 large Pack moist smoked salmon; thinly sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix together cream cheese, butter, pepper, and lemon juice in a bowl or food processor.
2. Spread tortillas, one at a time, with the cream cheese mixture.
3. Lay smoked salmon slices slightly overlapping over the cream cheese on each tortilla, leaving one edge uncovered (this will help hold the roll when sealed).
4. Place fresh dill sprigs or dill pickle spears along one edge of the tortilla.
5. Starting at the edge with the dill or pickles, tightly roll up the tortilla to seal it.
6. Wrap the rolled tortillas tightly in plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least one hour, up to 24 hours.
7. When ready to serve, unwrap the rolls and slice them about 1/2 inch thick on a diagonal.
TURKEY BREAST PINWHEELS WITH SUNDRIED TOMATO SPREAD
Same as above, except substitute very thinly sliced turkey breast for the salmon, omit lemon juice from cream cheese mixture and add 1/4 cup oil-packed sundried tomatoes, finely chopped; use watercress in place of dill. You may have tortillas left over, use the amount of spread for each which gives a uniform covering with no tortilla showing through, but also not too thick!! If you like capers, they are wonderful added to the spread for the Salmon pinwheels.
